topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation related to the management and taxation of oil and gas extraction from the outer continental shelf.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to declare that any royalty under an oil and gas lease...
Increases the minimum royalty under such leases from 12.5% to 20%.

topic.name: Tax Credit Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code that introduce or modify tax credits and penalties aimed at supporting small businesses, caregivers, education, and renewable energy initiatives.

Document 9: Increase the rates and maximum allowable amount of the tax credit for employer-provided child care facilities.
Document 10: Allow a tax credit for 50% of any contribution to a tax-exempt scholarship funding trust.

topic.name: STEM Education and Support
topic.description: The documents focus on various legislative efforts to enhance participation, access, and resources in STEM education at different educational levels.

Document 1: Broadening Participation in STEM Education Act
increase the participation of minority students in STEM fields
Document 3: STEM Gateways Act
encourage interest in the STEM fields
Document 5: INSPIRES Act
support local educational agencies in modernizing facilities for STEM education
Document 4: New Era Expansion Act
worker training in the wind energy industry and energy efficient construction
Document 6: S.O.S. Act of 2016
train teachers regarding mental health conditions in children
Document 7: LEARN Act
improve the literacy of children from birth through grade 12
Document 8: Improving No Child Left Behind
revise programs to improve academic standards
Document 9: SKILLs Act
Improving Literacy through School Libraries grant program
Document 10: TEACH Act
develop accessibility guidelines for electronic instructional materials

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative amendments and policies related to the Medicare program, addressing coverage, payment rules, and healthcare services.

Document 1: Shingles Prevention Act - Amends part B of title XVIII (Medicare) to provide for coverage of the shingles vaccine.
Document 2: Amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to modify payment rules and provisions related to the Medicare and Medicaid programs.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to revise payment rules for graduate medical education (GME) costs.
Document 4: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to provide for the consolidated coverage of home infusion therapy.
Document 5: Amends part D of title XVIII (Medicare) to require transparency in contracts with prescription drug plan sponsors.
Document 6: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to establish new separate fee schedule areas for physicians' services.
Document 7: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) with respect to prescription drug plans to grant authority to negotiate contracts with manufacturers.
Document 8: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) to require disclosure of information on hospital and ambulatory surgical center prices and quality.
Document 9: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) to provide medical assistance to former foster care children.
Document 10: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to specify that a 'physician' includes a licensed chiropractor.

topic.name: Suspension of Tariffs
topic.description: The documents focus on the temporary suspension or extension of duties on various chemicals and products under the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on placebos
used in clinical trials for the drug ASKP1240
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on mixtures of indoxacarb
inert ingredients
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on carbonic acid
dimethyl ester, polymer with 1,6-hexanediol
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on hydrazine hydrate
aqueous solution
Document 5: extend duty-free treatment under the Generalized System of Preferences
through June 30, 2012
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on benzyl carbazate
hydrazinecarboxylic acid, phenylmethyl ester
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic or modacrylic stable fibers
processed for spinning
Document 8: suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic fiber tow
Document 9: reduce temporarily the duty on 5-Methylpyridine-2,3-dicarboxylic acid dimethyl ester

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing various forms of support and benefits to veterans and their families.

Document 1: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to entitle an eligible employee to up to 12 workweeks of leave during any 12-month period because of the death of a son or daughter.
Applies the same leave entitlement to federal employees.
Document 2: Extends through 2013 eligibility for the concurrent receipt of military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation for veterans who were retired or separated due to physical disability.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to establish a pilot program to award grants to nonprofit organizations that primarily serve veterans or low-income individuals.
Requires such grants to be used to rehabilitate and modify the primary residence of disabled or low-income veterans.
Document 4: Amends federal military retired pay provisions to permit veterans with a service-connected disability of less than 50% to concurrently receive both retired pay and disability compensation.
Eliminates provisions requiring a phase in between January 1, 2004, and December 31, 2013, of concurrent receipt of retired pay and disability compensation.
Document 5: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 (FMLA) to cover employees at worksites that employ fewer than 50 employees.
Allows an employee covered by FMLA to take up to 4 hours during any 30-day period, and up to 24 hours during any 12-month period, of parental involvement leave.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to develop and carry out a program of support services for family and non-family caregivers providing noninstitutional extended care to disabled veterans.
Requires such support services to include educational sessions, stipends, and access to support services.
Document 7: Increases from $4,100 to $6,800 the amount authorized to be paid by the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) for improvements and structural alterations for homes of veterans with service-connected disabilities.
Document 8: Assigns a priority status for Medal of Honor recipients equal to that of former prisoners of war or Purple Heart recipients with respect to the provision of veterans' hospital care and medical services.
Document 9: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to reimburse any full-time board-certified health professional for continuing professional education expenses incurred, up to $1,600 per year.
Document 10: Reauthorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) homeless veterans reintegration programs.
Directs the Secretary of Labor to make grants to programs and facilities that provide dedicated services for homeless women veterans and homeless veterans with children.

topic.name: Child Protection and Trafficking Prevention
topic.description: Legislation focused on protecting children from abuse, neglect, and trafficking while enhancing state and local response mechanisms.

Document 1: require the state agency to demonstrate policies for identifying and screening any child who is a victim of sex trafficking
requires a permanency plan for a child age 14 or older to be developed in consultation with the child
Document 2: require grants under the DNA identification program to pay the salaries of employees of DNA criminal laboratories
increase and extend through FY2014 the authorization of appropriations for the DNA identification program
Document 3: authorize the Attorney General to make grants for supervised visitation in situations involving domestic violence
ensure that by January 1, 2016, a court-appointed special advocate shall be available to every victim of child abuse or neglect
Document 4: require the Office of Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention's annual grant to operate a cyber tipline for reporting child sexual exploitation
require each state reporting on missing children to provide a recent photograph of the child
Document 5: repeal a provision ensuring governmental social service agencies access to national crime information databases
Document 6: allow foster care maintenance payments to be made on behalf of a child in a residential family treatment center
evaluate and report to Congress on foster care maintenance payments
Document 7: authorize the use of grants to local educational agencies for child sexual abuse awareness and prevention programs
establish periodic evaluation requirements for such grant programs
Document 8: prohibit federal funding to organizations that fail to report known or suspected incidents of child sexual abuse
require states receiving grants to mandate reporting of child abuse and neglect
Document 9: expand definitions for purposes of the Violence Against Women Act
authorize appropriations for grants to combat violent crime against women
Document 10: require a state plan for child protective services to include identification of children known or suspected to be victims of sex trafficking
a child is considered to be a victim of 'child abuse and neglect' if identified as a victim of sex trafficking

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at protecting consumers in financial transactions, particularly in relation to mortgages and financial disclosures.

Document 1: prohibits the Department of the Army from recouping a bonus
provides financial assistance to a member who is facing financial hardship
Document 2: prohibits enforcement against any person of integrated disclosure requirements
good faith effort to comply with them
Document 3: raises the examination threshold that brings an insured depository institution within its supervisory purview
subject to the Act's reporting requirements
Document 4: specify an extenuating circumstance for determining if a borrower has an adverse credit history
mortgage loan delinquency or foreclosure
Document 5: require annual financial disclosure reports to include mortgages secured by real properties
reporting of any liability consisting of such a mortgage
Document 6: prohibits commencement of a foreclosure action unless specified requirements are met
requires borrower notification and assistance
Document 7: requires prompt notifications and decisions regarding short sale requests
aggrieved individual may bring a civil action
Document 8: amends the Truth In Lending Act to limit the annual percentage rate
maximum rate permitted by the laws of the state
Document 9: require rules of the SEC to permit an insured depository institution to elect not to provide mandatory certifications
Document 10: requires prompt notifications and decisions regarding a written request for a short sale
aggrieved individual to bring a civil action

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Tribal Affairs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, acquisition, and trust status of federal lands, particularly in relation to Native American tribes and their rights.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal land to Turnabout Ranch.
Modifies the boundaries of the Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monument.
Document 2: Amends the Wild Free-Roaming Horses and Burros Act to allow state or tribe management of wild horses and burros.
Directs the Department of the Interior and USDA regarding management functions.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to take additional lands into trust for the Siletz Tribe.
Prohibits gaming on lands taken into trust pursuant to this Act.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to negotiate agreements regarding property tax revenues lost due to land taken into trust.
Ensures full payment for property tax revenues lost as a result of lands being held in trust.
Document 5: Amends the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act to place restrictions on gaming on lands taken into trust.
Prohibits gaming on such lands unless certain criteria are met.
Document 6: Requires land in Nevada to be held in trust for the Duckwater Shoshone Tribe.
Prohibits class II or class III gaming on such land.
Document 7: Requires annual reports on the implementation of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act.
Bars funds awarded from being allocated for attorney fees until documentation is provided.
Document 8: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians.
Directs the Secretary to acquire trust title to land for the Tribe.
Document 9: Authorizes the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement involving the Hualapai Tribe.
Directs the Secretary of Interior to execute agreements related to water rights.
Document 10: Permits land held in trust for the Kalispel Tribe to be leased for terms greater than 25 years.
Includes specific tribes permitted to lease restricted lands without Secretary approval.

topic.name: Campaign Finance and Transparency
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at increasing transparency and regulating campaign financing for political candidates in the United States.

1: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971
require any candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
2: provides certified participating House of Representatives candidates with payments
amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to provide certified participating House of Representatives candidates with payments
3: prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls
directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to revise the do-not-call registry provisions
4: require the principal campaign committee of a candidate for election to the Office of President to include with the committee's statement of organization a copy of the candidate's birth certificate
amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971
5: allows a refundable credit of 50% of qualified congressional House campaign contributions
amends the Internal Revenue Code (IRC) to allow a refundable credit
6: prohibits the use by a recipient or its subsidiary of Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP) funds under the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 for lobbying expenditures
requires the Secretary of the Treasury to make such reports and certifications publicly available online
7: prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls
directs the Federal Trade Commission to revise the do-not-call registry provisions
8: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require each candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
the bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to authorize the FEC to disclose to the public the applicable tax returns
9: revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission (FEC)
this requirement shall now apply to any political committee
10: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more
requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy
topic.description: This cluster addresses various aspects of U.S. immigration legislation, including pathways to legal status, enforcement measures, and adjustments to immigration processes.

1: Development, Relief, and Education for Alien Minors Act of 2007 or DREAM Act of 2007 - Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional permanent resident status, an alien who...
Sets forth the conditions for conditional permanent resident status, including...
2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make a state or local subdivision ineligible for state criminal alien assistance program (SCAAP) funding if such state or subdivision...
3: Amends the Afghan Allies Protection Act of 2009 to extend the Afghan special immigrant visa program through FY2015...
4: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to declare that all deposits into the Immigration Examinations Fee Account shall remain available to the Department of Homeland Security...
5: Suspends until January 21, 2013, authority under the Immigration and Nationality Act for...
6: Sets forth border control provisions, including provisions respecting...
7: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to establish an employment eligibility verification system (EEVS)...
8: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to share immigration information with the Attorney General...
9: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to allow an alien widow of a U.S. citizen who was married for less than two years at the time of the citizen spouse's death...
10: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to authorize the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to admit into the United States a qualifying Canadian citizen over 50 years old...

topic.name: Military and Intelligence Oversight
topic.description: Legislation focused on the accountability, funding, and oversight of military and intelligence activities in the United States.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Identifies countries pursuing EMP weapons programs.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Defense to transfer members of the Armed Forces to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs for outpatient care.
Directs joint information provision on outpatient care services available through the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to ensure pay for Armed Forces during funding gaps.
Prohibits transfer of funds after December 31, 2011.
Document 4: Amends the National Security Act to require annual assessments of private contractors in the intelligence community.
Prohibits private contractors for certain government custody activities.
Document 5: Directs the Department of Defense to enhance security for military personnel at installations in the U.S.
Requires submission of a plan to Congress.
Document 6: Prohibits the use of private contractors for certain activities under government control.
Requires annual assessments of contractor use in the intelligence community.
Document 7: Directs the President to develop a comprehensive strategy for security in Pakistan.
Authorizes appropriations for Pakistan security initiatives.
Document 8: Amends the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act to enhance the functions of the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board.
Allows for whistleblower complaints related to privacy concerns.
Document 9: Authorizes appropriations for intelligence activities across various U.S. agencies.
Specifies personnel authorizations for the intelligence community.
Document 10: Authorizes appropriations for intelligence activities for FY2008.
Allows for employment of civilian personnel beyond authorized numbers for intelligence functions.

topic.name: Drug Pricing and Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving drug pricing transparency, ensuring safety in pharmaceuticals, and enhancing patient information regarding medications.

Document 1: require manufacturers of certain drugs to report price increases
subject to a civil penalty for non-compliance
Document 2: direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to promulgate regulations regarding patient medication information
requires scientifically accurate and plain language for drug information
Document 3: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish minimum standards for postmarketing data
improve transparency of pharmaceutical data
Document 4: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to disqualify certain generic drug applicants
expands the definition of 'first applicant' for generic drugs
Document 5: require HHS to establish a program to approve antibacterial drugs for limited populations
conduct postapproval monitoring programs for antibacterial drugs
Document 6: require labeling regulations for food containing trans fats
prohibit labeling that indicates zero trans fat when it contains a small amount
Document 7: require restaurant chains to disclose nutritional information on menus
provide additional nutritional information upon request
Document 8: establish the Advanced Diagnostics Education Council for innovative diagnostics
extend exclusivity periods for drugs related to diagnostic tests
Document 9: require approval applications for medical devices to include pediatric population data
establish a publicly accessible database for medical device studies
Document 10: require notification of adulterated food products to health authorities
provide authority for mandatory recalls of food products

topic.name: Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at improving mental health and substance abuse services across various populations, including veterans and college students.

Document 1: require the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA) to award grants to institutions of higher education to provide and improve mental and behavioral health services
expand outreach and training on college and university campuses.
Document 2: increase the penalties for such operator distribution of a controlled substance to a person under age 21
use proceeds from disposition of such property to be used for controlled substance monitoring programs in the states and for block grants to states for community mental health services.
Document 3: payments for up to three months to a covered veteran's landlord, mortgage company, or utility company for amounts of rent or mortgage that are in arrears
ensure that, when the payments begin, the veteran is in receipt of supportive services to further prevent homelessness.
Document 4: develop, implement, or enhance veteran's treatment courts
programs that involve continuing judicial supervision over nonviolent offenders with substance abuse or mental health problems.
Document 5: support services for early childhood educators and early childhood care and education programs
encourage cross-sector training opportunities for teachers and staff working in Head Start programs, Early Head Start programs, child care programs, early intervention programs.
Document 6: issue guidance for the provision of an in-person mental health screening for each member of the Armed Forces
identify post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), suicidal tendencies, and other behavioral health issues.
Document 7: require the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to design national strategies and implement programs to address chronic homelessness
Priority for grants is given to applicants that target services to individuals and families who have experienced more homelessness.
Document 8: requires any medical advisory opinion issued to a civilian board for the correction of a military record
to include the opinion of a clinical psychologist or psychiatrist if the request for the correction of the records concerned relates to a mental health disorder.
Document 9: provide a course of education to family members and caregivers of veterans on matters relating to coping with veterans' mental health disorders
establish a program to provide peer support to family members and caregivers of veterans.
Document 10: award grants to eligible institutions of higher education to improve mental and behavioral health services
convene an interagency, public-private sector working group to plan, establish, and begin coordinating and evaluating a targeted public education campaign.

topic.name: Rural Development and Agriculture
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at promoting rural development, agricultural assistance, and food security in rural and urban areas.

Document 1: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 to revise the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing.
...has a serious lack of mortgage credit for lower and moderate-income families.
Document 2: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to make biorefinery assistance available for renewable chemicals projects.
Defines 'renewable chemical' as a monomer, polymer, plastic, formulated product, or chemical substance produced from renewable biomass.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to conduct a pilot program under which the Secretary will give not more than three participating states the option of receiving a grant...to purchase fresh fruits and vegetables for distribution to schools.
...participating in food service programs under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act and the Child Nutrition Act of 1966.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to establish an urban agriculture outreach program to award grants to eligible entities.
...to support urban agricultural production.
Document 5: Provides for the inclusion of locally or regionally produced agricultural food products under specified agricultural, rural development, agricultural research, crop insurance, and nutrition programs.
Directs the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation to offer a whole farm adjusted revenue risk management insurance plan.
Document 6: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to conduct a pilot program...to purchase fresh fruits and vegetables for distribution to schools.
...for food service programs under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act and the Child Nutrition Act of 1966.
Document 7: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 to revise the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing.
...if, as under current law, such area has a population of between 10,000 and 25,000.
Document 8: Authorizes the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to provide financial assistance to the Housing Assistance Council to develop the capacity of community-based housing development organizations.
...to carry out community development and affordable housing activities for low- and moderate-income families.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Agriculture Improvement and Reform Act of 1996 to include shellfish as an eligible crop in the noninsured crop disaster assistance program.
...to include shellfish producers in the emergency assistance for livestock program.
Document 10: Amends the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 to eliminate the provision making a federal, state, or local government employee who is dismissed for participating in a strike...ineligible for the supplemental nutrition assistance program.

topic.name: U.S. Iran Relations and Sanctions
topic.description: Legislative measures focused on diplomatic relations, sanctions, and military contracts pertaining to Iran.

Document 1: Directs the President to appoint a high-level U.S. representative or special envoy for Iran
seek to ease tensions and normalize relations between the United States and Iran through bilateral and multilateral negotiations.
Document 2: prohibits funds available to the Department of Defense (DOD) from being used to procure goods or services from any person that provides material support to a covered Iranian person

requires the Federal Acquisition Regulation to be revised to require a person with a prospective contract to certify that the person does not engage in prohibited conduct with a covered Iranian person.
Document 4: require sanctions on Iran and related entities and persons for actions to acquire or develop ballistic missiles
the President shall block and prohibit all transactions in property and property interests in the United States of any identified person.
Document 6: eliminate authority to waive sanctions relating to transportation of crude oil from Iran
requires the imposition on violators of specified sanctions under the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996.
Document 9: prohibits the President from issuing a license that permits a person to conduct an offshore U.S. dollar clearing system for transactions involving the government of Iran
the President may impose additional sanctions pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.
Document 10: authorize the President to utilize the Proliferation Security Initiative and other measures to enforce all U.S. laws and Executive Orders

requires the President to report periodically to Congress on the identity of all known countries that have engaged in the import into or export from Iran of items useful to any nuclear, biological, chemical, missile, or conventional arms program.

topic.name: Social Security and Retirement Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on reforms and adjustments to Social Security benefits and retirement savings for individuals, particularly seniors and low-income earners.

Document 1: removes the limitation on the amount of outside income which a beneficiary may earn
Document 2: require taxpayers who are claiming the refundable portion of the child tax credit to include their social security account number
Document 3: match deposits made by low-income individuals into certain savings accounts
Document 4: establish a Social Security Surplus Protection Account to hold the Social Security surplus
Document 5: suspends for calendar 2008-2010 the minimum required distributions from certain individual retirement plans
Document 6: revise cost-of-living computation requirements for Social Security beneficiaries
Document 9: repeal government pension offset requirements applicable to insurance benefits
Document 10: restrict the application of the windfall elimination requirement to individuals whose combined monthly income exceeds a specified amount

topic.name: Aviation Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This cluster discusses various regulations and legislative acts aimed at improving safety, operational practices, and financial structures within the aviation industry.

Document 1: Authorizes the state of Ohio to use grant funds for intercity passenger rail service capital projects, for any other transportation projects.
pursuant to appropriations under title XII of the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009
Document 2: requires each air carrier providing passenger air transportation to train flight attendants and pilots to combat human trafficking
The Federal Aviation Administration shall report recommendations for improving the identification and reporting of human trafficking
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to establish an Aviation Rulemaking Committee to develop recommendations to minimize flight attendant fatigue.
Requires the Committee to comprise aviation safety experts, labor representatives, and industry stakeholders.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to complete a rulemaking that prohibits air carriers from charging fees for carry-on baggage
Requires air carriers to notify air passengers of baggage restrictions before they arrive at the airport.
Document 5: Repeals the prohibition against an air carrier's operation of an aircraft nonstop in air transportation between Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport and another airport
Prohibits the obligation of federal funds to enforce the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey perimeter rule.
Document 6: This bill reauthorizes and revises the Airport Improvement Program.
eliminates the federal cap on passenger facility charges (local user fees) of $4.50 per enplanement.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend increased excise taxes on aviation fuels and air transportation of persons and property.
Increases the authorization of appropriations for airport planning and development.
Document 8: Prohibits the screening of a minor passenger by a pat down search without consent and presence of a parent.
Requires presence of a representative of the air carrier for unaccompanied minors.
Document 9: Prescribes a special rule to require the Secretary of Transportation to apportion amounts for airport planning and development based on passenger boardings.
Specifies conditions for airports with fewer than 10,000 passenger boardings.
Document 10: Prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from implementing any change to the prohibited items list that would permit passengers to carry small, non-locking knives.
Ensures security measures are maintained at airports.

topic.name: Fisheries Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at the conservation, management, and regulation of fisheries in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to provide...
seaward boundary of a coastal state's jurisdiction in the Gulf of Mexico...
Document 2: amends the Saltonstall-Kennedy Act to revise the program under which the Department of Commerce provides financial assistance for fisheries research and development projects.
Commerce must carry out a national program of fisheries research and investment that supports rebuilding and maintaining healthy U.S. fish populations...
Document 3: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to require the Secretary of Commerce...to annually make available to the New England Fishery Management Council...
Document 4: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Reauthorization Act of 2006 to prohibit the requirement that a mechanism be established for specifying annual catch limits...
Document 5: requires the Department of Transportation (DOT), in coordination with the Department of the Navy, to report to Congress on the program for disposal of government-owned merchant vessels...
Document 6: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to repeal a provision that requires any fishery management plan...
Document 7: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to add the state of New York as a member of the New England Fishery Management Council.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the South Atlantic red snapper fishery and report to Congress.
Document 9: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds...
Document 10: Prohibits a person from: (1) shipping, transporting, offering for sale, selling, or purchasing genetically altered salmon...

topic.name: Labor Rights and Protections
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at improving labor rights, protections against discrimination, and fair employment practices.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to make the restrictions on oppressive child labor inapplicable to employment of an employee age 16 or 17
in a logging or mechanized operation owned or operated by that parent or person.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to award back pay and additional liquidated damages
to any employee against whom an employer has discriminated.
Document 3: Authorizes an employee to request from an employer a change in the terms or conditions of the employee's employment
Makes it unlawful for an employer to interfere with any rights provided to an employee under this Act.
Document 4: Amends the portion of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 known as the Equal Pay Act
Revises remedies for, enforcement of, and exceptions to prohibitions against sex discrimination in the payment of wages.
Document 5: Prohibits covered entities from engaging in employment discrimination on the basis of an individual's actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity
Declares that it shall be unlawful for an employer, because of an individual's actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity, to discriminate.
Document 6: The services of the Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service (FMCS) shall be used
Requires any issue as to whether such requirement applies to an arbitration clause to be determined by federal law.
Document 7: Makes unenforceable any clause of an agreement between and employer and employee requiring arbitration of a dispute arising under the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act
Protects under USERRA an individual who is absent from employment in order to receive medical or dental treatment.
Document 8: Authorizes an employee to request from an employer a change in the terms or conditions of the employee's employment
Makes it unlawful for an employer to interfere with any rights provided to an employee under this Act.
Document 9: Amends the Consumer Credit Protection Act to define 'assignment' to exclude payroll deductions or preauthorized payment plans
No employer may take any adverse employment action against an employee because the employee's earnings have been subject to garnishment.
Document 10: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unfair labor practice for an employer to recognize or bargain collectively with a labor organization
that has not been selected by a majority of the employees in a secret ballot election.

topic.name: Communications Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to telecommunications, broadcasting, and internet services in the United States.

Document 1: DTV Transition Assistance Act - Amends the Digital Television Transition and Public Safety Act of 2005
U.S. households may obtain coupons toward the purchase of a television antenna.
Document 2: Kelsey Smith Act - Amends the Communications Act of 1934
telecommunication carriers to provide call location information to law enforcement agencies.
Document 3: Restoring Confidence in Our Democracy Act - Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971
rules for electioneering communications, especially targeted communications.
Document 4: Internet Freedom Preservation Act - Amends the Communications Act of 1934
establish certain Internet neutrality duties for broadband service providers.
Document 5: Next Generation Television Marketplace Act - Repeals provisions of the Communications Act of 1934
regulation of rates and broadcast signal carriage.
Document 6: Permanent Internet Tax Freedom Act of 2007 - Amends the Internet Tax Freedom Act
permanent ban on state and local taxation of Internet access.
Document 7: Broadband for First Responders Act of 2010 - Amends the Communications Act of 1934
increase the electromagnetic spectrum allocation for public safety services.
Document 8: DTV Cliff Effect Assistance Act of 2009 - directs funding for digital TV translators
fill gaps in the digital coverage for full-power stations.
Document 9: Improving Rural Call Quality and Reliability Act of 2016
requires intermediate providers to register with the FCC.
Document 10: Four Corners Television Access Act of 2009 - Amends federal copyright law
allow satellite or cable retransmission of signals of broadcast television stations.

topic.name: Privacy Legislation and Protection
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing the protection of personal and sensitive information across different domains.

1: prohibit a provider of remote computing service or electronic communication service to the public from knowingly divulging to any governmental entity the contents of any communication
Requires a law enforcement agency, within 10 days after receiving the contents of a customer's communication, to provide the customer a copy of the warrant
2: prohibit researchers from disclosing such information from federally funded research to persons not connected to the research
Disclosures of such information are permitted if required by law, necessary for the medical treatment of the research subject
3: Requires any entity that collects, sells, discloses for consideration, or uses personally identifiable information to provide consumers notice before using personally identifiable information for unrelated purposes
establish a privacy policy with respect to the collection, sale, disclosure for consideration, dissemination, use, and security of consumers' personally identifiable information
4: Reaffirms the authority of the Comptroller General to audit and evaluate financial transactions, programs, and activities of elements of the intelligence community
Requires the Comptroller General to establish safeguards to protect the unauthorized disclosure of all classified and other sensitive information
5: Reaffirms the authority of the Comptroller General to audit and evaluate financial transactions, programs, and activities of elements of the intelligence community
Requires the Comptroller General to establish safeguards to protect the unauthorized disclosure of all classified and other sensitive information
6: provide criminal penalties for intentional failures to provide required notices of a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information
requires a person who owns or possesses data in electronic form containing a means of identification to provide prompt notice to the U.S. Secret Service or FBI
7: directs the Inspector General of each executive agency to submit a report that includes a description of the logical access standards used by the agency
a description of agency policies and procedures for ensuring that entities that provide services to the agency are implementing data security management practices
8: prohibit the Department of Education from funding educational agencies that release student educational records without written parental consent
expands the list of individuals exempt from such prohibitions
9: Requires review by the Supreme Court of appeals of judgments challenging the legality of the President's Surveillance Program
Requires the Supreme Court to advance on the docket any such appeal and expedite it to the greatest extent possible
10: requires a person who holds, uses, or discloses protected health information to permit an individual to inspect and copy the information
establish safeguards and procedures to ensure the privacy, confidentiality, security, accuracy, and integrity of such information

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection and management of wildlife species and their habitats in the United States.

1: Amends the Lacey Act Amendments of 1981 to prohibit any person from importing, exporting, transporting, selling, receiving, acquiring, purchasing in interstate or foreign commerce, breeding, or possessing any prohibited wildlife species.
Defines 'breeding' as facilitating the propagation or reproduction of a prohibited wildlife species.
2: This bill amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to replace the current standard for awarding court costs in citizen suits.
3: Provides that the determination by the United States Fish and Wildlife Service of the threatened status of the polar bear under the Endangered Species Act of 1973 shall have no force or effect.
5: Amends the Wetlands Loan Act to increase and extend funding for conservation of migratory waterfowl and habitat essential to the preservation of such waterfowl.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the funds provided pursuant to this Act should be used for preserving and increasing waterfowl populations.
6: Amends the Great Sand Dunes National Park and Preserve Act to provide that the purpose of the Baca National Wildlife Refuge shall be to restore, enhance, and maintain wetland, upland, riparian, and other habitats for native wildlife.
7: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to prohibit treatment of the Gray Wolf as an endangered or threatened species.

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support, funding, and resources for small businesses and manufacturing sectors in the United States.

Document 1: establish a Federal Acceleration of State Technologies Deployment Program
distribute matching federal funds to qualifying states
Document 2: authorize the National Institute of Standards and Technology to establish a program
designate up to 25 institutions of higher education as U.S. manufacturing universities
Document 3: authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration to make $4 billion in guarantees
Document 4: establish a program to provide support to SBA regional, district, and branch offices
carry out a rural lending outreach program
Document 5: amend the Federal Credit Union Act to prohibit certain member business loans
develop a tiered approval process for member business lending
Document 6: make the E-Verify program permanent
increases civil and criminal penalties for specified hiring-related violations
Document 7: requires the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to identify and publish qualified census tracts
increase the low-income housing income tax credit
Document 8: establish and maintain an Internet website that provides information on benefits for veterans
consolidate and improve information relating to such benefits
Document 9: amend the Small Business Act to exclude publicly traded businesses from small business goals
publish a report regarding federal prime contracts awarded to businesses identified as small businesses
Document 10: amend the Small Business Act with respect to obtaining collateral for disaster loans
reduce paperwork burdens on small businesses applying for SBA disaster assistance loans

topic.name: Government Property and Community Development
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures related to the conveyance of government property and initiatives aimed at community development and public safety.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to convey to Derry Township, Pennsylvania, all interest of the United States in a parcel of real property
to permit the township to use such parcel for a regional police headquarters and other public purposes.
Document 2: Reauthorizes programs for combating criminal gangs
Establishes or increases funding for criminal gang-related programs.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to award grants, on a competitive basis and with federal matching funds, to qualified local building code enforcement departments

topic.name: Voting and Election Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing the integrity, accessibility, and transparency of the electoral process in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 (HAVA) to revise requirements for the audit capacity of voting systems
Requires the use of an individual, durable, voter-verified paper ballot
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to reduce the maximum amount of contributions that any person may make to any candidate
Requires adjustment to such amount for inflation after 2018
Document 4: Prohibits an authorized committee of a candidate who is a Member of Congress from accepting contributions from any entity for which the candidate sought a congressional earmark
Prohibits the acceptance of any contribution from a separate segregated fund established and administered by a corporation or labor organization
Document 5: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 (HAVA) to revise requirements for the audit capacity of voting systems
Requires the voting system to provide the voter with an opportunity to correct any system-made error in the voter-verified paper ballot
Document 9: Requires states to carry out congressional redistricting in accordance with a process under which members of the public are informed of redistricting proposals
Requires each state redistricting entity to establish and maintain a public Internet site meeting specified requirements
Document 10: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit a corporation from making a disbursement of funds in connection with a campaign for election
Prohibits disbursement of funds for six years after conviction of certain offenses

topic.name: U.S. Trade and Economic Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of U.S. trade laws, regulations, and policies, particularly concerning countervailing duties, trade evasion, and relations with countries like Cuba and China.

Document 1: prohibits the President from modifying, terminating, abandoning, or transferring the lease with the government of Cuba
the United States acquired 45 square miles of land and waters that currently contain Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba
Document 2: amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to expand the authority of the administering authority or the International Trade Commission (ITC) to impose countervailing duties
includes exchange-rate misalignment (undervaluation of a foreign currency) by a country as a countervailable subsidy
Document 3: imposition of countervailing duties on imports into the United States from a country subsidizing
merchandise from a nonmarket country, unless the administering authority cannot identify and measure subsidies
Document 4: establish within the Office of International Trade of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) a Trade Remedy Law Enforcement Division
prevent and counter evasion of antidumping or countervailing duty orders
Document 5: establishes U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP), in the Department of Homeland Security (DHS)
develop jointly and submit to Congress a Joint Strategic Plan for enforcing U.S. customs and trade laws
Document 6: require the Secretary of the Treasury to work with the head of each agency participating in the International Trade Data System
develop and maintain the necessary information technology infrastructure to support the operation of the ITDS
Document 7: amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to expand the authority of the administering authority or the International Trade Commission (ITC)
requires the administering authority to use methodologies that measure certain benchmarks
Document 8: makes an alien who returns to Cuba after admission or parole into the United States ineligible for such status adjustment
subjects the alien to all the provisions of the Immigration and Nationality Act
Document 9: directs the U.S. Trade Representative (USTR) to report to Congress annually on the estimated annual loss of revenue to holders of U.S. intellectual property rights
impose duties on merchandise from China in an amount equivalent to the estimated annual loss
Document 10: amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to repeal the embargo on trade with Cuba
authorizes the President to impose export controls with respect to Cuba

topic.name: Native Hawaiian and Territorial Governance
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures related to governance, education, and rights of Native Hawaiians and other U.S. territories.

1: Amends the Native Hawaiian Education Act to revise the duties and composition of the Native Hawaiian Education Council.
Requires the Council to hold at least one community consultation each year on each of the islands of Hawaii.
6: Amends the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 and the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992 to extend through FY2017 the authorization of appropriations for housing assistance for Native Hawaiians.
Extends federal recognition to the government as the representative governing body of the Native Hawaiian people upon election of officers and certification by the Secretary.
7: Reaffirms that the United States has a special political and legal relationship with Native Hawaiians.
Establishes the Native Hawaiian Interagency Coordinating Group to coordinate federal programs and policies that affect Native Hawaiians.
8: Establishes the U.S. Office for Native Hawaiian Affairs within the Office of the Secretary of the Interior.
Recognizes the right of the Native Hawaiian people to adopt organic governing documents.
4: Requires the head of each federal agency to report to the Comptroller General annually describing federal funds distributed by such agency for the preceding fiscal year.
Prohibits the distribution of such funds unless in such distribution each of the territories is treated in the same manner as each of the states and the District of Columbia.

topic.name: Abortion and Health Care Policy
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses legislation related to abortion, health care access, and the rights of individuals and organizations in relation to abortion services.

Document 1: Spina Bifida Health Care Program Expansion Act - Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to provide comprehensive health care coverage
Document 2: Protecting Life in Funding Education Act or PRO-LIFE Act - Amends the General Education Provisions Act to prohibit the provision of federal education funding to state or local educational agencies that make health services available to students through school-based health centers
Document 4: Unborn Child Pain Awareness Act of 2010 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to require an abortion provider who knowingly performs an abortion of a pain-capable unborn child
Document 5: Health Care Conscience Rights Act - Amends title I of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to declare that nothing in that title requires an individual to purchase individual health insurance coverage that includes coverage of an abortion
Document 6: Title X Abortion Provider Prohibition Act - Amends the Public Health Service Act to prohibit the Secretary of Health and Human Service (HHS) from providing any federal family planning assistance to an entity unless the entity certifies that, during the period of such assistance, the entity will not perform, and will not provide any funds to any other entity that performs, an abortion
Document 8: Stop Abortion Funding in Multi-state Exchange Plans Act or SAFE Act - Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to require the Director of the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to ensure that no multi-state qualified health plan offered in a health benefit exchange provides coverage of abortion
Document 10: Access to Contraception for Women Servicemembers and Dependents Act of 2014 - Expands the TRICARE health care program managed by the Department of Defense (DOD) to entitle additional female beneficiaries and dependents to care related to the prevention of pregnancy.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, workforce management, and financial practices of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS).

Document 1: Requires the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to determine, on an annual basis, the amount of any surplus contributions by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to the Federal Employees' Retirement System (FERS)
Allows USPS to provide voluntary separation incentive payments of up to $25,000 to USPS employees who voluntarily separate before October 1, 2015.
Document 2: Directs the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to issue a Families of Fallen Heroes Semipostal Stamp.
Requires 50% of the proceeds from the sale of such stamp to be transferred to the Tragedy Assistance Program for Survivors.
Document 3: Directs the U.S. Postal Service (USPS), as an employer, to maintain a workforce of officers and employees necessary to meet its responsibilities with respect to homeland security.
including the rapid residential delivery of medical countermeasures in the event of a biohazard attack against the United States.
Document 4: Amends provisions concerning the Federal Employees' Retirement System (FERS), with respect to employees of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS).
Provide for the use of excess FERS contributions to pay USPS debt obligations and make pension contributions.
Document 5: Makes it the policy of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to ensure reasonable and sustainable workloads and schedules for supervisory and management employees.

States that any changes in, or termination of, pay policies and schedules and fringe benefit programs for members of the recognized supervisors' organization shall be done in accordance with a specified consultation process.
Document 6: Makes it the policy of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to ensure reasonable and sustainable workloads and schedules for supervisory and management employees.

States that any changes in, or termination of, pay policies and schedules and fringe benefit programs for members of the recognized supervisors' organization shall be done in accordance with a specified consultation process.
Document 7: Directs the U.S. Postal Service (USPS), in making a determination whether to close or consolidate a post office, to consider the economic effects on the community served by such post office.
Increases from 60 to 120 days the waiting period during which the USPS is prohibited from taking action to close or consolidate a post office.
Document 8: Expands the specific powers of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to include the provision of basic financial services.
Including: small-dollar loans; checking accounts, interest-bearing savings accounts, and services relating to international money transfers.
Document 9: Revises the eligibility requirements for air carriers to be selected by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to transport nonpriority bypass mail in the state of Alaska.
Redefines 'existing mainline carrier' as a mainline carrier that, for each of the preceding 36 months, while having met certain certification and qualification standards.
Document 10: Amends provisions of federal law relating to the governance, workforce, and financing of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS).
Authorizes USPS to establish a general, nationwide mail delivery schedule of five days per week.

topic.name: Radiation and Health Compensation
topic.description: Legislation addressing compensation and health programs related to radiation exposure and mining industries.

Document 1: Amends the Radiation Exposure Compensation Act to extend the Radiation Exposure Compensation Trust Fund
Increases the amount of compensation an individual filing a claim may receive to $150,000.
Document 5: Includes within the definition of a 'radiation-risk activity' for purposes of eligibility for veterans' disability compensation the exposure at any time to ionizing radiation
Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to include in regulations pertaining to the service-connection of veterans' disabilities additional provisions to ensure...

topic.name: Commemoration and Memorialization Acts
topic.description: Legislation focused on establishing memorials and honoring the service and sacrifice of various groups including emergency medical service providers, veterans, and law enforcement officers.

Document 1: establish the National Emergency Medical Services Memorial
honor the commitment and ultimate sacrifice of emergency medical service providers.
Document 3: issue an annual proclamation calling on the people of the United States to observe two minutes of silence on Veterans Day
in honor of the service and sacrifice of veterans throughout the history of the nation.
Document 4: establish the National Global War on Terrorism Memorial
to commemorate and honor the members of the Armed Forces who served on active duty in support of the Global War on Terrorism.
Document 6: family of a deceased law enforcement officer to request a U.S. flag
in honor of the officer
Document 7: prohibits any policy that would restrict or prevent an association member from displaying a Service Flag
in respect to members serving in the Armed Forces during any period of war or hostilities.
Document 10: issue an annual proclamation calling on the people of the United States to observe two minutes of silence on Veterans Day
in honor of the service and sacrifice of veterans throughout the history of the nation.

topic.name: Workplace Safety and Staffing Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring safe working conditions and appropriate staffing levels in various industries, particularly healthcare and mining.

Document 1: Registered Nurse Safe Staffing Act of 2011 requires hospitals to implement staffing plans for nursing services.
Establishes a hospital nurse staffing committee to oversee staffing plans.
Document 2: Mine Worker Safety Hotline Accessibility Act requires mine operators to provide hazard reporting hotline information.
Facilitates the reporting of mine accidents and safety concerns.
Document 3: Safer Oil and Natural Gas Drilling Technology Research and Development Act directs the Secretary of Energy to improve safety in drilling operations.
Focuses on operational safety and reducing environmental impacts in oil and gas exploration.
Document 6: Nurse Staffing Standards for Patient Safety and Quality Care Act mandates staffing ratios for registered nurses.
Requires hospitals to provide staffing plans and conduct audits for compliance.
Document 9: Ensuring Worker Safety Act amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act to evaluate state safety plans.
Requires assessment of state plans for effectiveness in preventing occupational injuries.
Document 10: Corporate Injury, Illness, and Fatality Reporting Act mandates large employers to report work-related deaths and injuries.
Requires accurate record-keeping and compliance data from large employers.

topic.name: Employment and Compensation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving employment conditions, compensation, and support for workers in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes the Agent Orange Illness Compensation Program to provide compensation for federal employees
Offsets such payment by any payment made on a claim based on the same illness, injury, or death
Document 4: Requires each employer who employs 100 or more employees to provide each employee one week of paid vacation
Requires the Secretary of Labor to conduct a public awareness campaign
Document 7: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account
Increases the figures in the basic EUC formula
Document 8: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to declare that an unlawful employment practice occurs when a discriminatory compensation decision is adopted
Allows liability to accrue for up to two years preceding the filing of the charge

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Education
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to environmental control, education funding, consumer protection, and judicial administration.

Document 1: Nutria eradication program under which the Secretary of the Interior may provide financial assistance to coastal states.
Management, research, and public education activities carried out in accordance with the United States Fish and Wildlife Service's document.
Document 2: Requires the District of Columbia to provide education savings accounts for children who are eligible to receive a free public education.
Funds that are adjusted based on the family's income and range from 80% to 90% of the funds that the District of Columbia Public School System would otherwise spend.
Document 3: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act to replace the position of Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau with a five-member Commission.
Prohibits the Chair of the Commission from making requests for estimates related to appropriations without the prior approval of the Commission.
Document 4: Imposes mandatory minimum prison terms for homicide, manslaughter, and kidnapping of federal judges and law enforcement officers.
Expands the penalties for assaulting or interfering with federal officers and employees.
Document 5: Makes permanent a temporary exemption from permit requirements of the National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System under the Federal Water Pollution Control Act.
Exemption applies to a vessel that is less than 79 feet in length or a fishing vessel for any discharge.
Document 6: Amends the Coquille Restoration Act to require the Department of the Interior to manage the Coquille Forest in accordance with the laws pertaining to the management of Indian trust land.
Removes a provision limiting available remedies to equitable relief.
Document 7: Amends the District of Columbia Home Rule Act to eliminate federally-imposed mandates over the District of Columbia's local budget process.
Repeals the federal cap on amounts the District may borrow.
Document 8: Requires that the principal office of the U.S. Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims be in the metropolitan area of Washington, DC.
Allows the President to remove judges violating that residency requirement.
Document 9: Amends the Johnson-O'Malley Act to establish a program through which the Bureau of Indian Affairs shall contract with eligible entities for educational benefits to Indian students.
BIA must ensure full geographic coverage and the full participation of eligible entities.
Document 10: Directs the President to appoint additional circuit judges for specified circuit courts of appeals.
Establishes a federal judicial district in the Virgin Islands.

topic.name: Immigration and Education Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments related to immigration and education, particularly in the context of integrating immigrants and enhancing educational programs.

1: amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
expand world language learning in the elementary grades
2: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
make up to 10% of the worldwide employment-based immigration level available to qualified immigrants
6: amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
establish a Grow Your Own Teacher program
8: establishes in the Executive Office of the President a National Office of New Americans
coordinate federal, state, and local efforts to ensure the economic, linguistic, and civic integration of immigrants

topic.name: Consumer Safety and Awareness Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at enhancing consumer safety and awareness regarding health risks and product labeling.

Document 1: Eating Disorders Awareness, Prevention, and Education Act of 2007
increase awareness of such disorders among parents and students
Document 2: Cleaning Product Right to Know Act of 2011
requires a cleaning product to bear a label listing each of its ingredients
Document 3: Cleaning Product Right to Know Act of 2014
requires a product that is not in conformity with the labeling requirements to be treated as a misbranded hazardous substance
Document 4: Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Prevention Act
requires the Consumer Product Safety Commission to publish safety standards for carbon monoxide alarms
Document 5: Center of Excellence in Prevention, Diagnosis, Mitigation, Treatment, and Rehabilitation of Military Eye Injuries
develop, implement, and oversee a registry for tracking the diagnosis, treatment, and follow-up for each case of eye injury
Document 6: Avonte's Law Act of 2014
authorize the Attorney General to make grants to law enforcement agencies to reduce the risk of injury and death relating to the wandering characteristics of individuals with autism
Document 7: Avonte's Law Act of 2015
provide education and resources to law enforcement agencies to reduce the risk of wandering
Document 8: Battlefield Excellence through Superior Training Practices Act
develop, test, and validate human-based training methods for treating combat trauma injuries
Document 9: Universal College Credit Act
allow a tax credit for qualified tuition and related expenses
Document 10: Toxic Metals Protection Act of 2010
prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution of children's products containing specified levels of toxic metals

topic.name: Disaster Relief and Resilience
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing relief and enhancing resilience in response to natural disasters and climate-related challenges.

Document 1: Defines 'hurricane-affected county' related to natural disaster declarations.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to provide emergency financial assistance to agricultural producers for crop and livestock losses.
Document 6: Requires community water systems to submit vulnerability assessments identifying threats from climate change and extreme weather.
Establishes a grant program for increasing the resiliency of community water systems to threats.

topic.name: Energy and Environmental Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to energy production, environmental protection, and resource management in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes the exploration, leasing, development, production, and transportation of oil and gas in and from the Coastal Plain in Alaska.
Amends the Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act to repeal the prohibition against production of oil and gas from Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.
Document 3: Authorizes the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to extend for up to three consecutive two-year periods the time period during which the licensee must commence construction of hydroelectric project.
Authorizes FERC to reinstate the license, effective as of its expiration date, if the existing period required for commencement of construction has expired before enactment of this Act.
Document 5: Amends the Water Desalination Act of 1996 to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to assist in research and development, environmental and feasibility studies.
Limits the federal share to 25% of the total cost of the project.
Document 9: Declares that no presidential permit shall be required for a specified pipeline application filed on May 4, 2012, by TransCanada Corporation.
Requires the President to establish the Transportation Fuels Regulatory Committee to analyze and report on the cumulative impacts of certain covered rules and actions under the Clean Air Act.

topic.name: Terrorism Legislation and Insurance
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures related to terrorism, including legal liabilities, insurance programs, and benefits for victims.

Document 1: amends the federal judicial code to narrow the scope of foreign sovereign immunity
authorize U.S. courts to hear cases involving claims against a foreign state for injuries, death, or damages that occur inside the United States as a result of a tort, including an act of terrorism
Document 2: extends the Terrorism Insurance Program through December 31, 2020
specifies the aggregate industry insured losses resulting from certified acts of terror which will trigger the federal share of compensation
Document 3: members of the Armed Forces who were killed or wounded in the attack at Fort Hood should receive the same treatment and benefits as the victims of the September 11 attacks
the Department of Defense should honor the Americans killed or injured in such attack with awards that recognize their sacrifices
Document 6: provides permanent resident status adjustment for an applicant alien who was lawfully present in the United States on September 10, 2001
deemed to be a beneficiary of the September 11th Victim Compensation Fund of 2001

topic.name: Public Health and Environmental Preservation
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative measures related to public health initiatives, environmental protection, and cultural preservation.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to acquire the Gold Hill Ranch in Coloma, California
interpret the history of the Wakamatsu Tea and Silk Farm Colony and related pioneer history associated with Japanese immigration
Document 3: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to reestablish the Program Office of the Gulf of Mexico Program
coordinate the actions of EPA and of other federal agencies and state and local authorities in developing strategies to improve the water quality
Document 5: directs the President to transmit to Congress and make publicly available a report on the status of the implementation of the National HIV/AIDS Strategy
report on the status of international progress towards achieving universal access to HIV/AIDS treatment
Document 6: establish an integrated, five-year Global HIV/AIDS-Free Generation Strategy
expand efforts to combat global HIV/AIDS
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish the Interagency Prostate Cancer Coordination and Education Task Force
develop a summary of advances in federal prostate cancer research
Document 9: Authorizes the Secretary of Health and Human Services to enter into an agreement under which an individual agrees to serve as a dentist for a not less than two years at a facility with a critical shortage of dentists
payment on the individual's dental education loans

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of federal legislative acts addressing various aspects of public policy including land protection, health care, environmental responsibility, housing finance, and economic development.

Document 1: Blue Ridge Parkway Protection Act - Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to acquire lands and interests in land from willing sellers
Requires the Secretary to submit a report to Congress which describes the condition of the Parkway
Document 2: America's Affordable Health Care Act of 2008 - Authorizes a health insurance issuer to apply to the Secretary of Health and Human Services to certify health insurance coverage policies
Requires the Secretary to review the impact of this Act on the availability and purchase of health insurance coverage.
Document 3: Stop Online Ammunition Sales Act of 2012 - Amends the federal criminal code to require the licensing of ammunition dealers
Prohibits any person except a licensed importer, manufacturer, or dealer from selling ammunition.
Document 4: Military Environmental Responsibility Act - Requires the Department of Defense (DOD) and defense-related agencies to fully comply with designated federal and state environmental laws
Waives any immunity of the United States with respect to such laws as applied to DOD.
Document 5: Mortgage Finance Act of 2015 - Appoints the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) receiver of the Federal National Mortgage Association
Establishes the MFA as an independent agency of the federal government to guarantee securities.
Document 6: Sewage Sludge in Food Production Consumer Notification Act - Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem as adulterated food that is produced on land on which sewage sludge was applied
States that nothing in this Act shall be construed to modify the prohibition on the use of sewage sludge in organic food production.
Document 7: Second Chance at Homeownership Act of 2012 - Amends the National Housing Act to establish in the Federal Housing Administration a Second Chance at Homeownership Program
Defines a 'second-chance borrower' as any mortgagor who has not been the mortgagor under any other mortgage.
Document 8: Access to Professional Health Insurance Advisors Act of 2011 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to exclude remuneration paid for licensed independent insurance producers
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to defer to the state's findings and determinations.
Document 9: Supporting Entrepreneurial Economic Development Act or SEED Act - Amends the Public Works and Economic Development Act of 1965 to allow a recipient of an economic adjustment grant to directly expend or redistribute grant funds
Defines 'business incubator' to mean an organization or entity established to foster the startup of businesses.
Document 10: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to prohibit any funds under a grant to a state for establishing an American Health Benefit Exchange
Prohibits funds from being used for a bonus or pay increase for executives.

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures related to federal regulations, including public health, environmental protection, and housing policies.

Document 1: prohibits the use of federal funds to finalize, implement, or enforce proposed rule
Standards for the Growing, Harvesting, Packing, and Holding of Produce for Human Consumption
Document 2: Enacts into law the final rule entitled Federal Implementation Plans
Requires compliance with such rule
Document 4: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Administrator of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to award grants
set forth training requirements for practitioners registered to prescribe or dispense methadone
Document 5: Prohibits an individual from purchasing or leasing any housing acquired using federal loan or grant funds
Requires each state, metropolitan city, and urban county to give highest priority to providing housing for disabled veterans
Document 7: Amends the Gulf of Mexico Energy Security Act to prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from offering any oil and gas exploration lease
Authorizes the Secretary to offer for leasing specified areas currently under moratorium

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Social Issues
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing social issues related to employee benefits, education, health, and veteran affairs.

1: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) to require the mandatory summary description of a group health plan.
Presumes that retiree health benefits cannot be modified or terminated as of the date an employee retires.
2: Treats the District of Columbia as a state for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives.
Increases membership of the House from 435 to 436 Members.
4: Authorizes the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to train school nurses.
Requires training to prepare school nurses to take necessary measures to protect and preserve life.
6: Directs the Secretaries of Defense (DOD) and Veterans Affairs (VA) to jointly operate the Federal Recovery Coordination Program.
Assists members of the Armed Forces with severe or catastrophic injuries or illnesses.
7: Makes the amount payable for programs of education at nonpublic institutions of higher education pursued by individuals enrolled in the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) post-9/11 educational assistance program.
Modifies guaranteed home loan fees for certain veterans.

topic.name: Legislative Measures on Rights and Protections
