topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing rights and protections for individuals in different contexts, including workplace rights, marriage equality, and military justice.

1: Prohibits the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) from making lobbying expenditures;
Declares that violating this Act shall constitute violation of federal law limiting the use of appropriated funds to influence certain federal contracting and financial transactions.
2: Protect Our Workers from Exploitation and Retaliation Act or the POWER Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exclude from the definition of 'immigrant' under such Act a nonimmigrant alien who files a petition for status if the Secretary of Homeland Security determines that the alien has suffered abuse or harm.
Authorizes the Secretary to permit an alien to temporarily remain in the United States and grant the alien employment authorization.
3: Establishes a Sexual Assault Oversight and Response Council as an independent entity from the chain of command of the Department of Defense.
Requires the Council to advise on the development of sexual assault reporting protocols, risk-reduction and response training, and policy.
4: Amends the Defense of Marriage Act to repeal a provision that prohibited a state from being required to recognize any public act, record, or judicial proceeding of any other state respecting a same sex marriage.
Amends the federal rules of construction concerning the definitions of 'marriage' and 'spouse'.
5: Waives a state's sovereign immunity with respect to the enforcement of uniformed services members' employment or reemployment rights under the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act.
Provides increased liquidated damages, and authorizes punitive damages, against state or private employer violations of USERRA.
6: Amends the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 to require the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board to appoint attorneys to serve as public interest advocates in proceedings before the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court.
Permits the FISC to request review of significant interpretations of FISA.
7: Amends the Defense of Marriage Act to repeal a provision that prohibited recognition of same sex marriages across states.
Removes the definition of 'spouse' as a person of the opposite sex.
8: This bill amends the Federal Credit Union Act to permit an examination to be carried out only once every 18 months of a federal credit union or an insured credit union that has total assets of less than $1 billion.
The NCUA shall issue a report on how this bill affects its budget.
9: Reaffirms that the United States is in an armed conflict with the Taliban, al Qaeda, and associated forces and that those entities continue to pose a threat to the United States.
Sets forth standards for habeas corpus review for any individual who is held by the United States at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay.
10: Requires trial only by a military commission for any foreign national who engages in an offense relating to a terrorist attack against persons or property in the United States.
Is subject to trial for that offense by a military commission under the Uniform Code of Military Justice.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Actions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various federal legislative acts aimed at addressing specific issues related to land management, information technology, foreign relations, and social welfare.

Document 1: Amends the Small Tract Act of 1983 to permit the sale, exchange, or interchange under such Act of National Forest System lands.
Requires any proceeds under this Act to be deposited in the special fund established under the Sisk Act.
Document 2: Expands the responsibilities of agency heads with respect to the acquisition of information technology (IT).
Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to ensure the effective operation of a website that provides monthly updated information on agency investment in IT projects.
Document 3: Prohibits federal funds from being expended, obligated, or used in any manner to support directly or indirectly operations in or over Libya.
Requires rescission of any obligated, but not expended, funds to support such operations.
Document 4: Amends the Trafficking Victims Protection Act of 2000 to authorize appropriations for FY2013-FY2017 to the Attorney General for protection and assistance for aliens who are victims of trafficking.
Amends federal criminal code provisions regarding transportation of a minor with intent to engage in criminal sexual activity.

topic.name: Government Programs and Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various government acts and programs aimed at improving public services, health care, water management, and financial relief for specific populations.

Document 1: Water Transfer Facilitation Act of 2009 - Provides that the following voluntary water transfers shall be considered to meet conditions specified for authorized transfers of Central Valley Project (CVP) water

Directs the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Director of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service and the Commissioner of Reclamation, to initiate and complete on an expedited basis programmatic documentation to facilitate voluntary water transfers
Document 2: Ensuring Access to Quality Complex Rehabilitation Technology Act of 2015

Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to cover, as medical and other health services, complex rehabilitation technology (CRT) items designed or individually configured for a specific qualified individual
Document 3: National Guard Bonus Repayment and Financial Relief Act
This bill requires the Army to review all bonuses, incentive pays, or similar benefits received by members of the Army National Guard between January 1, 2004, and December 31, 2010
Document 4: This bill amends the Safe Drinking Water Act by requiring small or medium public water systems to sample their tap water for lead and copper
Public water systems must notify their customers of lead concentration levels in drinking water when the lead levels exceed those regulatory limits
Document 5: Bicycle and Pedestrian Infrastructure Improvement Act of 2015
Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to establish a pilot program to make loans and loan guarantees to eligible entities to carry out bicycle and pedestrian infrastructure projects
Document 6: Arkansas Valley Conduit Act of 2009 - Conditions the construction by the Secretary of the Interior of the Arkansas Valley Conduit, Colorado, on a contract providing for the payment within a specified period of 35% of the Conduit's cost

Requires revenue derived from contracts for the use of Fryingpan-Arkansas project excess capacity or exchange contracts using project facilities to be credited towards payment of the actual cost of Ruedi Dam and Reservoir
Document 7: CHIP Extension and Improvement Act of 2015
Revises and extends through FY2019 at generally increased levels the program under title XXI (State Children's Health Insurance)
Document 8: Palliative Care and Hospice Education and Training Act
This bill amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants or contracts for Palliative Care and Hospice Education Centers
Document 9: Primary Care Physician Reentry Act of 2015
This bill directs the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a demonstration program to facilitate physician reentry into primary care clinical practice
Document 10: Requires a state approving agency (agency) that receives an application from an educational institution for approval of a course of education for purposes of the veterans' educational assistance program to respond in writing to such institution within 90 days
Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to review and, if appropriate, overturn a negative agency determination

topic.name: Energy and Infrastructure Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative acts related to energy production, infrastructure maintenance, and maritime safety in the United States.

Document 1: Reliable Investment in Vital Energy Reauthorization Act or the RIVER Act
reauthorize through FY2025 the program of hydroelectric production incentives
Document 2: Corps of Engineers Reform Act of 2011
establish a program to make grants to states to carry out harbor maintenance and deepening projects
Document 3: Bulk-Power System Reliability Impact Statement Act
require reliability coordinators registered with the Electric Reliability Organization (ERO) to report regarding the reliability and affordability of electricity
Document 4: Short Sea Shipping Promotion Act of 2009
exempt from the harbor maintenance tax cargo contained in intermodal cargo containers
Document 6: United States Mariner and Vessel Protection Act of 2009
deploy a maritime safety and security team for up to six months to deter, protect against, and rapidly respond to acts of piracy
Document 7: congressional policy that a state has authority to manage the waters of rivers
maintains authority to allocate the quantity of water in the reservoir
Document 8: amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to repeal the coal and related technologies program
establish a coal technology program encompassing research and development
Document 9: Ports-to-Forts Act of 2012
make grants to states or port authorities to cover the direct or indirect costs for repair or construction of commercial strategic seaports

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing accountability, oversight, and efficiency within various government programs and agencies.

Document 1: directs the Financial Stability Oversight Council to compile and submit to the Secretary of the Treasury a list of entities that the Council deems Too Big To Fail
break up entities on the List so that their failure would no longer cause a catastrophic effect upon the U.S. or global economy
Document 2: require the participation of the President, Vice President, each Member of Congress, each political appointee, and each Congressional employee in state Exchanges established for the purchase of health care coverage
Document 4: establishes the Federal Agency Program Realignment and Closure Commission to evaluate each executive agency and its programs
submit to the President and Congress a list of the agencies and programs it recommends eliminating or realigning
Document 5: requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to submit annually to Congress a financial report containing an interagency crosscut budget for restoration activities
an accounting of amounts received and obligated by each federal agency for restoration activities
Document 6: directs the Inspector General of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to submit quarterly reports to Congress on the IG's activities with respect to Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac
their activities and condition while they are in conservatorship status
Document 9: establishes within the Veterans Health Administration the position of Director of Physician Assistant Services
requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to ensure that an individual is serving in such position no later than 120 days after the enactment of this Act

topic.name: Governmental Policy and Infrastructure Development
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts focused on governmental policies and initiatives aimed at infrastructure development, public safety, and regional economic improvement.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to include a report on interagency counternarcotics strategy for the region
Requires federal assistance through current counternarcotics programs to be provided in the Region
Document 2: Establishes a Southeast Crescent Authority for all parts of the following southeastern states not eligible for assistance
Directs the Authority to perform various functions, including formulating plans and programs to spur economic development
Document 3: Prohibits the government from approving an amendment to the Washington Metropolitan Area Transit Authority Compact unless its signatories certify qualifications
The Department of Transportation (DOT) shall ensure that the next three federal appointments to the Board have such qualifications
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish a national cemetery in the Sarpy County, Nebraska, region to serve the needs of veterans and their families
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Commissioner of Reclamation, to plan, design, and construct a regional water system
Authorizes New Mexico and Santa Fe County, in agreement with the Pueblos, to modify the extent, size, and capacity of the County Distribution System
Document 6: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to provide competitive grants to establish and maintain centers of excellence for green infrastructure
Defines 'green infrastructure' as a stormwater management technique that preserves, restores, enhances, or mimics natural hydrology
Document 7: Establishes in the legislative branch the Afghanistan-Pakistan Study Group which shall conduct an independent assessment of the situation in Afghanistan and Pakistan
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Group should be modeled on the Iraq Study Group
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers a new tax credit for qualified emergency position-indicating radio beacon
A 'qualified emergency position-indicating radio beacon' is a device installed on an eligible boat that transmits distress signals
Document 9: Directs the Attorney General to establish national, regional, and local public awareness campaigns focused on promoting the advantages of continued education among youth
Document 10: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the definition of 'renewable biomass' to include slash and precommercial sized thinnings harvested in environmentally sustainable quantities
Biomass obtained on land in any ownership from the immediate vicinity of any building at risk from wildfire.
